question_text: Why do electric motors get hot during use?

options.label: A
options.text: Because all the electrical energy is converted to motion

options.label: B
options.text: Because some electrical energy is converted to heat due to friction and resistance

options.label: C
options.text: Because motors are designed to produce heat

options.label: D
options.text: Because the power supply is too strong

correct_answer: Because some electrical energy is converted to heat due to friction and resistance
explanation: Electric motors are not 100% efficient. When electrical energy passes through the motor's coils, some is lost as heat energy due to electrical resistance in the wires. Additionally, friction between the moving parts of the motor produces more heat. This is why motors get warm during use - it's wasted energy that isn't being converted into useful motion.
